title: “Dependent on attachment, suffering arises. All the various sufferings in the world Come into being through attachment. The fool, by making attachments, Again and again…
content:
“Dependent on attachment, suffering arises.
All the various sufferings in the world
Come into being through attachment.
The fool, by making attachments,
Again and again encounters suffering.
Therefore, the wise one, knowing,
Does not create attachments,
Contemplating the nature of birth and suffering.

Those who fall into the stream
Of repeated birth and death,
Going from one existence to another,
Are deluded by ignorance.

This great delusion, ignorance,
Leads to long wandering —
But beings who have attained true knowledge
Do not go to renewed existence.”

title: Elder Nun Sundarī Nandā: Nandā, look at this body — Diseased, impure, stinking. Develop the mind on foulness, One-pointed, well-concentrated. As this body, so that…
content:
Elder Nun Sundarī Nandā:

Nandā, look at this body —
Diseased, impure, stinking.
Develop the mind on foulness,
One-pointed, well-concentrated.

As this body, so that body —
As that body, so this body —
Foul, rotting —
Only fools delight in it.

Thus contemplate this body,
Day and night without fatigue.
With your own wisdom,
Therefore escape.
With your own wisdom,
Escape pride in beauty,
And see truly
What fools see.

This city is built of bones,
Plastered with flesh and blood.
Within dwell aging and death,
Conceit and hypocrisy.

Without heedlessness,
Rightly contemplate that body —
The true nature of this body
Is seen inside and out.
I am disenchanted with the body,
My mind is dispassionate.
Without heedlessness, free from bonds,
I am at peace, stilled.

title: Standing to one side, the deity Tayana recited this verse in the presence of the Blessed One: O brahmin, striving, cut the stream, Abandon and…
content:
Standing to one side, the deity Tayana recited this verse in the presence of the Blessed One:

O brahmin, striving, cut the stream,
Abandon and destroy
All clinging to sensual desires.
Without cutting sensual desires,
A sage cannot attain unification of mind.
If you do what should be done,
Be steadfast and energetic.
If a goer-forth is lazy,
He scatters even more dust.
Not doing is better than doing badly —
Doing badly leads to suffering afterward.
Having done, do it well —
Doing well brings no suffering.
Like holding grass the wrong way,
It can cut your hand,
So too, the ascetic life wrongly practiced
Drags one to hell.
Any lazy conduct,
Any defiled practice,
Any evil action in the holy life
Does not yield great fruit.

title: - “Lust, Venerable Bharadvāja, is the mind. Sometimes, even toward those who are like mothers, lust arises. Toward those like sisters, lust arises. Toward those…
content: - “Lust, Venerable Bharadvāja, is the mind. Sometimes, even toward those who are like mothers, lust arises. Toward those like sisters, lust arises. Toward those like daughters, lust arises. Bharadvāja, is there another cause, another reason why these young monks … live the holy life completely?” - “Great king, the Blessed One, the Knower, the Seer, the Arahant, the Perfectly Enlightened One, has said: ‘Come, monks, contemplate this body from the soles of the feet upward, from the top of the head downward, enclosed by skin and full of various impurities. In this body there are: head hairs, body hairs, nails, teeth, skin, flesh, sinews, bones, bone marrow, kidneys, heart, liver, diaphragm, spleen, lungs, intestines, intestinal mesentery, stomach, feces, bile, phlegm, pus, blood, sweat, fat, tears, skin oil, saliva, snot, synovial fluid, urine.’ This, great king, is the cause, this is the reason why these young monks live the perfectly pure holy life for their whole life, completing it fully.”

title: “When ignorance leads the way, monks, for the attainment of unwholesome states, then lack of conscience and lack of fear of wrongdoing follow. For one…
content:
“When ignorance leads the way, monks, for the attainment of unwholesome states, then lack of conscience and lack of fear of wrongdoing follow. For one overwhelmed by ignorance and without wisdom, monks, wrong view arises. For one with wrong view, wrong intention arises. For one with wrong intention, wrong speech arises. For one with wrong speech, wrong action arises. For one with wrong action, wrong livelihood arises. For one with wrong livelihood, wrong effort arises. For one with wrong effort, wrong mindfulness arises. For one with wrong mindfulness, wrong concentration arises.

When true knowledge leads the way, monks, for the attainment of wholesome states, then conscience and fear of wrongdoing follow. For one with true knowledge and wisdom, monks, right view arises. For one with right view, right intention arises. For one with right intention, right speech arises. For one with right speech, right action arises. For one with right action, right livelihood arises. For one with right livelihood, right effort arises. For one with right effort, right mindfulness arises. For one with right mindfulness, right concentration arises.”

title: “And, Carpenter, where are these unwholesome thoughts abandoned without remainder? Their abandoning has also been taught. Here, Carpenter, a monk, secluded from sensual desires, secluded…
content: “And, Carpenter, where are these unwholesome thoughts abandoned without remainder? Their abandoning has also been taught. Here, Carpenter, a monk, secluded from sensual desires, secluded from unwholesome states, enters and dwells in the first jhāna, accompanied by initial and sustained thought, with rapture and pleasure born of seclusion. Here, those unwholesome thoughts are abandoned without remainder.”

title: “Monk, when a person dwells with sense faculties unguarded, without moderation in eating, not committed to wakefulness, not watching over wholesome states, not devoted to…
content: “Monk, when a person dwells with sense faculties unguarded, without moderation in eating, not committed to wakefulness, not watching over wholesome states, not devoted to developing the factors of enlightenment at the beginning and end of the night — then his body feels intoxicated, his eye does not see directions clearly, the Dhamma is not remembered by him. Sloth and torpor overcome his mind and remain. Without gladness, he lives the holy life. He has doubts about the true Dhamma. Therefore, monk, you should train thus: ‘I will guard the sense faculties, have moderation in eating, be committed to wakefulness, watch over wholesome states, and be devoted to developing the factors of enlightenment at the beginning and end of the night.’”

title: “Monks, the perception of foulness, when developed and cultivated, is of great fruit, great benefit, merging with the deathless, having the deathless as its end.…
content: “Monks, the perception of foulness, when developed and cultivated, is of great fruit, great benefit, merging with the deathless, having the deathless as its end. For what reason is this said? When a monk dwells much with the perception of foulness, his mind retreats, turns back, and draws away from sexual intercourse, and thus his mind is not strained, becomes peaceful, and disenchantment arises. Just as, monks, a cock’s feather or a sinew thrown into fire retreats, turns back, draws away, and is not strained — so too, when a monk dwells much with the perception of foulness, his mind retreats, turns back, draws away from sexual intercourse, and thus his mind is not strained, becomes peaceful, and disenchantment arises. If a monk dwells much with the perception of foulness, yet his mind still runs after, gets stuck in sexual intercourse, and disenchantment does not arise, then he should understand: ‘The perception of foulness is not developed in me; there is no distinction before and after in me; I have not attained the fruit of development.’ There he is clearly aware. If a monk dwells much with the perception of foulness, his mind retreats, turns back, draws away from sexual intercourse, his mind is not strained, becomes peaceful, and disenchantment arises, then he should understand: ‘The perception of foulness is developed in me; there is a distinction before and after in me; I have attained the fruit of development.’ There he is clearly aware. That the perception of foulness, when developed and cultivated, is of great fruit, great benefit, merging with the deathless, having the deathless as its end — for this reason it is said.”

title: - What is called the wrong path? What destroys day and night? What defiles the holy life? What is a bath without water? - Greed…
content:
- What is called the wrong path?
What destroys day and night?
What defiles the holy life?
What is a bath without water?
- Greed is called the wrong path,
Aging destroys day and night,
Women defile the holy life,
Ensnaring humankind —
Austerities and the holy life
Are a bath without water.

title: “And what, monks, is the origination of the world? Dependent on the eye and forms, eye-consciousness arises. The meeting of the three is contact. With…
content: “And what, monks, is the origination of the world? Dependent on the eye and forms, eye-consciousness arises. The meeting of the three is contact. With contact as condition, feeling. With feeling as condition, craving. With craving as condition, clinging. With clinging as condition, existence. With existence as condition, birth. With birth as condition, aging, death, sorrow, lamentation, pain, grief, and despair arise. This is the origination of the world. Dependent on the ear … on the nose … on the tongue … on the body … Dependent on the mind and phenomena, mind-consciousness arises. The meeting of the three is contact. With contact as condition, feeling. With feeling as condition, craving. With craving as condition, clinging. With clinging as condition, existence. With existence as condition, birth. With birth as condition, aging, death, sorrow, lamentation, pain, grief, and despair arise. This, monks, is the origination of the world.”
